_comment: WC2026 Group K, MD3. Standings before: Colombia 6, Portugal 4, DR Congo 1 (1-1 Portugal, 0-1 Colombia), Uzbekistan 0 (1-3 Colombia, 0-4 Portugal). DR Congo (1 pt) MUST WIN to reach 4 pts and chase one of the 8 best-third spots -- and because the best-third tiebreak runs on GOAL DIFFERENCE, they are motivated to win by a margin. This is a legitimate STRUCTURAL incentive (qualification + GD matters), the Senegal archetype, so we DO lift DR Congo -- not a speculative motivation guess. Uzbekistan are eliminated (0 pts, GD -6, a best third is out of reach) and were hammered (conceded 7 in two games), but NO over-downgrade (South Africa lesson): a broken side can still compete. DR Congo the better and more motivated team; Uzbekistan leaky. Home = Uzbekistan, away = DR Congo. Cards not modelled (referee TBD).
_thirds_context: DR Congo is the only live best-third angle here: a WIN takes them to 4 pts, which on current numbers would slot into the qualifying third band (the cutline sits around 3 pts GD -1) -- and since thirds are separated by GD then GF, the MARGIN matters, so DR Congo should chase goals, not just the win. A draw or loss eliminates them. Uzbekistan cannot reach a best third even by winning (GD -6 is unrecoverable), so they play for pride only.

meta.stakes: DR Congo (1 pt) must win -- ideally by a margin -- to chase a best-third spot (decided on goal difference). Uzbekistan (0 pts, GD -6) are eliminated and play for pride. DR Congo the better, motivated side; Uzbekistan leaky.

home.name: Uzbekistan
notes: Weak; lost both, conceded 7. attack 0.85 (no over-downgrade -- South Africa lesson).
Leaky, and DR Congo will push. defense 1.12.

away.name: DR Congo
notes: MUST WIN by a margin for a best third -> legitimate STRUCTURAL lift, context 1.08 (not a motivation guess).
Decent defensively vs a weak Uzbekistan. defense 0.95.

_lineups._comment: Confirmed/projected XIs (RotoWire/Sports Mole/SI, ~60 min pre-KO). They VALIDATE the calibration: coach Desabre is reported to abandon the back five for a FOUR-man defence 'given the need for goals' -> DR Congo go more attacking, exactly the structural must-win-by-margin thesis behind the context 1.08 lift. The side is talented (Wan-Bissaka, Mbemba, Masuaku; Bakambu + Wissa up top). Uzbekistan (Cannavaro) are fully fit but on a four-match losing run and weak (conceded 7), set up 3-4-3 with Shomurodov. No lambda change: the lineups confirm DR Congo as the motivated, goal-hungry favourite and Uzbekistan as the leaky, eliminated side.

_postmatch.result: Uzbekistan 1-3 DR Congo. DR Congo win and -- crucially -- by the margin they needed: they finish 3rd in Group K on 4 pts, GD +1, GF 4, and qualify as one of the best thirds (currently the TOP-ranked third). Uzbekistan finish last (0 pts) and out.
_postmatch.verdict: 1X2 hit and the STRUCTURAL read fully vindicated -- the Senegal archetype again. DR Congo had to win by a margin to chase a best third (GD is the tiebreak), I applied the legitimate structural lift (context 1.08) and flagged the confirmed switch to a back four 'for goals' as validation, and they delivered a 3-1 with the healthy GD that secured a strong third. They scored 3 vs lambda 1.38 (finishing upside tail), so the margin overperformed xG, but the side, the must-win-by-margin thesis and the qualification consequence all landed.
_postmatch.lesson: Reconfirms the STRUCTURAL-incentive rule (qualification + GD matters -> lift, attack) as the legitimate, hard adjustment, distinct from speculative motivation. DR Congo's strong third (4 pts, +1) then RAISED the best-thirds cut-line to Iran (3/0/3) and knocked South Korea out -- a reminder that a late strong third reshapes the whole cut-line, which is why the Austria-Algeria third-place math had to be recomputed once K finished.
